title: clinopodium vulgare subsp. orientale
intro: Clinopodium vulgare subsp. orientale, also known as the Mountain Calamint, is a subspecies of the wild mint plant, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and digestive-aiding compounds. The health benefits of Clinopodium vulgare subsp. orientale include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular choice in herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
